title: quadlet: Support Type=oneshot container files
body:
These just run once and are considered successful at exist. Not much is needed to support it, but we have to avoid overwriting the type with Type=notify.

Signed-off-by: Alexander Larsson <alexl@redhat.com>


```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
Onesot is fundamentally different than notify and are used for different things. oneshot just means something that runs and when it exits the "thing" is done and the service is considered activated. Typically used for various kinds of intialization of things. Since synchronization is at process exit there is no need for sd-notify here.

 I think it makes sense to be able to do such things also in a container. For example it may be a container with a db-client app that ensures the database tables are updated to the latest version.
Hmm, i guess we should also not do -d (detach) for oneshot.

> LGTM

Ah, I still desire a system test.
New version has a system test.

Preferrable a test that works.

LGTM
